Implement the design at .tmp/missions/results/hook-repo-name-design.md with ONE simplification: NO special-casing of any directory. The rule is exactly: spoken repo name = the repo of the session's cwd. Steps: (1) hook_payload.py carries cwd:Path|None on every payload, parsed from the stdin 'cwd' field, improving method_ratio off 0.75; (2) hooks.py *_cmd handlers resolve find_config_dir(payload.cwd) and gate on it returning None (no config there -> silent, which is natural not a special case), and thread the resolved dir into handle_stop so read and vibe-write agree; (3) the seven shell hook scripts extract cwd from stdin via jq mirroring subagent.sh, fix the gate to ${cwd}/.punt-labs/vox/vox.md|vox.local.md, and DELETE the dead mcp-proxy/serve.port relay branch entirely (PL-PP-1); (4) when cwd is absent fall back to existing Path.cwd() default in find_config_dir, do not add suppression logic; (5) no punt-labs or parent special-case anywhere. Tests: cwd present->repo name; cwd in subdir->resolves the repo; cwd missing->None payload field; gate matches vox.md not config.md; no script references mcp-proxy/serve.port/config.md after edit. Commit incrementally one commit per logical step each passing make check; do not accumulate more than 30 minutes uncommitted. make check (incl check-oo, mypy, pyright, shellcheck) must pass; OO ratchet must not regress. voxd logs a cache HIT and a cache MISS distinctly at INFO on the synthesize path, including the cache file path (stderr-only in daemon mode; no print()).

The synthesize/unmute path surfaces a `cached` (hit/miss) boolean back through the client to the MCP unmute (and/or status) response, so a caller can programmatically confirm a cache hit. You choose the exact field name and which response(s) carry it; keep the daemon/client protocol change minimal and typed (update types.py, not an ad-hoc dict).
Caching behavior is UNCHANGED: same cache key (text+voice+provider MD5), same hit/miss decision, same audio. This is observability only — no perf regression on the hot path.

Tests: a cache MISS then an identical cache HIT assert the `cached` flag flips false->true and the HIT does not re-synthesize; the boundary (daemon response -> client -> MCP result) is exercised, not just the in-process cache. Cover the miss path and a provider/voice difference (different key -> miss).

Distinctness: music.py defines a curated tuple of AT LEAST 12 music-variation descriptors that meaningfully span instrumentation / tempo / texture / mood-nuance (e.g. warm analog pads vs crisp arpeggios vs deep sub-bass groove vs airy plucks — draft real, musically-distinct phrases, not filler). vibe_to_prompt() takes a variation index and inserts the descriptor as a prompt layer. Empty/None variation must reproduce today's prompt exactly (back-compat for existing callers/tests).

generate() passes variation = the current (vibe,style) pool size (0-based), so the Nth new track for a pool gets the Nth variation descriptor and the 12 fill distinct variations. Cycle (index % len) if the pool ever exceeds the descriptor count.

Playlist rotation: a POOL_SIZE constant (=12). In skip_next() and the vibe-change / turn_on new-generation paths, group saved tracks by the <vibe_slug>-<style_slug>- prefix (add a generator method like tracks_for(vibe, style)). If pool size >= POOL_SIZE, select by shuffle a track != the last-played one, set _track + _replay so the loop plays it with ZERO ElevenLabs API calls; else generate a new track. Add a _last_pool_track (or index) scheduler slot to avoid back-to-back repeats. MusicLoop must NOT need changes.

Naming: generated track names must not collide at minute resolution (today's auto_track_name is <vibe>-<style>-YYYYMMDD-HHMM, so 2 generations in one minute overwrite and the pool never grows). Include a short unique/variation suffix so a pool reliably grows to 12 distinct files.
Behavior preserved: /music on --name replay, /music play <name>, gapless handoff, and the off path are unchanged. Rotating an established pool makes no API call (assert this).

Tests (exercise the scheduler domain, not just helpers): pool fills to 12 with each generation using a distinct variation index; the 13th on/next ROTATES (no generate/API call — mock the generator/provider and assert call count 0 on rotation); shuffle never returns the immediately-previous track; a different vibe or style is a separate pool; <12 still generates. Mock the ElevenLabs provider — never hit the real API in tests.
